name: Fort Leavenworth
tagline: The 'Intellectual Center of the Army,' home of the Command and General Staff College on the Missouri River.
_note: Army PME hub (Leavenworth KS, on the Missouri River, ~30 mi NW of Kansas City; Central time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA KS102 Fort Leavenworth; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Home of the Command and General Staff College (CGSC); the oldest active Army post west of Washington; also the U.S. Disciplinary Barracks (military prison). Mid-career officer + family throughput. Medical: Munson Army Health Center. On-post public schools: Fort Leavenworth USD 207. Facts verified from official sources (home.army.mil/leavenworth, Munson AHC, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Kansas is TRICARE West. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 66048 (Leavenworth).

data.Location: Leavenworth, KS (Missouri River, ~30 mi NW of Kansas City)
data.Role: Home of the Command and General Staff College (CGSC)
data.History: The oldest active Army post west of Washington, D.C.
data.Also home to: The U.S. Disciplinary Barracks (the military's prison)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Leavenworth is the 'Intellectual Center of the Army,' home to the Command and General Staff College, where mid-career officers study before their next level of leadership. Most families here are on a one-year academic tour or supporting it as faculty and staff.

On the Missouri River about 30 miles northwest of Kansas City, it's the oldest active Army post west of Washington, with a historic, settled feel, and it's also home to the U.S. Disciplinary Barracks.

sections.title: A One-Year School Tour: On-Post Is Popular

data.subtitle: Most families come for a one-year CGSC course, which shapes the housing choice: on-post (with its well-regarded schools) is very popular, with Leavenworth and the KC metro as off-post options.
intro: Because the typical tour is about a year, many families take on-post housing for the convenience and the on-post public schools (USD 207), which are popular. The cost of living is affordable.
Off post, the town of Leavenworth is closest, and some families choose the Kansas City metro (including the Kansas suburbs) if they don't mind a longer commute.

recommendations.advice: On-post housing and the USD 207 schools are very popular for the one-year tour; get on the list as early as you can.
Leavenworth borders the post with affordable housing and a short commute.
The Kansas City suburbs (e.g., the Kansas side) offer more amenities for those who prefer a longer commute.
With a short tour, weigh the simplicity of on-post housing against setting up a household off post.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Fort Leavenworth site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Fort Leavenworth has privatized on-post family housing across several historic and newer neighborhoods, very popular with CGSC families for the convenience and the on-post schools.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA KS102 (Fort Leavenworth)

sections.title: Schools
data: Fort Leavenworth has its own well-regarded on-post public school district (Fort Leavenworth USD 207) serving students who live on post. Off post, Leavenworth and other area districts serve families who live there.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and specialty care on post; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via Kansas City-area network hospitals

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and at Fort Leavenworth the typical one-year CGSC tour usually tips the scale toward on-post housing or renting.
rent: Your tour is the one-year CGSC course, so buying rarely pencils out.
You prefer on-post housing for the convenience and the USD 207 schools.
You want flexibility for a short tour.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent if you go off post.
buy: You have a longer faculty or staff tour and plan to stay.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable Leavenworth or KC market fits your budget for a home you like.
You're comfortable with resale timing on a longer tour.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but a one-year CGSC tour usually favors on-post housing or renting, while longer faculty and staff tours can make buying worth a look. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-post lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: On-post housing is very popular for the one-year tour and the USD 207 schools; get on the list as early as possible.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Kansas vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact USD 207 (on post) or your local district early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Munson Army Health Center or network providers.

data.q: What is Fort Leavenworth known for?
data.a: It's the home of the Command and General Staff College, the 'Intellectual Center of the Army,' where mid-career officers study. It's the oldest active Army post west of Washington and also home to the U.S. Disciplinary Barracks.

data.q: How long is a typical tour?
data.a: Most families come for the roughly one-year CGSC course, which is why on-post housing and renting are so common.
On-post housing (with the well-regarded USD 207 schools) is very popular; off post, Leavenworth is closest and the Kansas City suburbs are an option.
Fort Leavenworth is MHA KS102. Use the calculator above for your grade.
